source_name: State and territory government net debt (Treasury budget papers, Pass 2B)
notes: Pass 2B value-entry update. 4 of 8 jurisdiction values loaded from official Treasury Budget Paper PDFs verified during this pass: NSW Budget Paper No. 1 Chapter 8 Table 8.1 (A$120.3b GGS), VIC Strategy and Outlook (A$167.6b GGS), QLD Budget Paper No. 2 Balance Sheet (A$41.8b GGS), and WA Budget Paper No. 3 Table 2 (A$39.0b Total Public Sector, not GGS - explicitly Partial coverage with concept caveat). SA, TAS, ACT and NT remain Partial coverage with null values; their Treasury budget homepages returned HTTP 403/503 from this build environment. No national aggregate is published.
source_rights: CC BY 4.0 where stated by publisher; each state/territory Treasury copyright applies
citation: State and territory budget papers (NSW Budget Paper No. 1; VIC Statement of Finances; QLD Budget Paper No. 2; WA Budget Paper No. 3; SA Budget Statement; TAS Budget Paper No. 1; ACT Budget Outlook; NT Budget Paper 2).

rows.row_label: Net Debt ($m), Table 8.1 Key balance sheet aggregates of the general government sector (June 2026 Budget = $120,275m)
rows.comparability_note: Verified from NSW 2025-26 Budget Paper No. 1 (Budget Statement), Chapter 8 Balance Sheet, Table 8.1. General government sector net debt at 30 June 2026 = $120,275m = A$120.3 billion. Conceptually comparable to VIC and QLD GGS net debt rows; not directly comparable to WA which publishes Total Public Sector as its headline.

rows.row_label: Net debt $ billion, general government sector, 2025-26 budget (= 167.6)
rows.comparability_note: Verified from Victoria 2025-26 State Budget Strategy and Outlook (Budget Paper No. 2), Chapter 4 Fiscal Outlook table. General government sector net debt at 30 June 2026 = $167.6 billion. Conceptually comparable to NSW and QLD GGS net debt rows; not directly comparable to WA which publishes Total Public Sector as its headline.

rows.row_label: Net debt ($m), general government sector, 2025-26 Budget (= 41,803)
rows.comparability_note: Verified from QLD 2025-26 Budget Paper No. 2 (Budget Strategy and Outlook), Chapter 5 Balance Sheet and Cash Flows. General government sector net debt at 30 June 2026 = $41,803m = A$41.8 billion (text confirms "$22.092 billion" for 2024-25 estimated and the next column in the time-series row is 41,803 for 2025-26 Budget). Conceptually comparable to NSW and VIC GGS net debt rows.

rows.debt_concept: Total Public Sector net debt (WA Treasury headline measure; not General Government Sector)
rows.row_label: Total Public Sector Net Debt at 30 June ($m), Table 2 Key Budget Aggregates (= 38,968)
rows.comparability_note: Verified from WA 2025-26 Budget Paper No. 3 (Economic and Fiscal Outlook), Table 2 Key Budget Aggregates. WA publishes Total Public Sector net debt ($38,968m at 30 June 2026 = A$39.0 billion) as its headline because WA still owns major utilities (Water Corp, Synergy, ports) unlike eastern states that have privatised them. WA BP3 reports general-government net debt only in directional terms ("rises by $5.5 billion over four years to 2028-29") without a clean single-year GGS dollar figure in its overview tables. This row is therefore NOT directly comparable to NSW/VIC/QLD GGS rows above and is recorded as Partial coverage with the WA-specific concept name preserved.

rows.row_label: Pending verification from named Budget Statement
rows.comparability_note: SA state budget page returned HTTP 403 to scripted access from this sandbox; figure must be hand-keyed from the Budget Statement (PDF) opened in a browser. Value left null pending verification.

rows.row_label: Pending verification from named Budget Paper No. 1
rows.comparability_note: TAS Treasury page returned HTTP 503 to scripted access from this sandbox; figure must be hand-keyed from Budget Paper No. 1 (PDF) opened in a browser. Value left null pending verification.

rows.debt_concept: General government sector net debt (ACT publishes a unified general government sector but boundary differs from larger jurisdictions)
rows.row_label: Pending verification from named Budget Outlook
rows.comparability_note: ACT Treasury page returned HTTP 403 to scripted access from this sandbox. ACT also has a smaller jurisdictional scope than mainland states, so its net-debt figure is not directly comparable to NSW/VIC/QLD/WA in absolute dollar terms. Hand-key from Budget Outlook (PDF) and preserve the scope-difference caveat. Value left null pending verification.

rows.row_label: Pending verification from named Budget Paper 2
rows.comparability_note: NT Treasury page returned HTTP 403 to scripted access from this sandbox; figure must be hand-keyed from Budget Paper 2 (PDF) opened in a browser. NT has a much smaller revenue base than mainland states; absolute dollar net debt is not directly comparable. Value left null pending verification.

fields.national_aggregate_note: No national aggregate is published. Even with NSW + VIC + QLD GGS rows verified, summing them with WA (Total Public Sector, not GGS) and with the still-null SA/TAS/ACT/NT rows would produce a misleading total. Concept boundaries and jurisdictional scope differ across the four verified rows already loaded; ACT and NT have much smaller jurisdictional scope than mainland states. Commonwealth AOFM debt is a separate measure on a separate card.
fields.comparability_basis: Preferred concept is general government sector net debt as published in each state/territory's latest Budget Paper / Statement (typically 30 June 2026 estimate from the 2025-26 budget cycle). NSW, VIC and QLD publish a clean GGS net debt headline (verified). WA publishes Total Public Sector as its headline measure because it still owns utilities; the WA row is recorded as Partial coverage with the WA-specific concept name preserved. SA, TAS, ACT and NT remain Partial coverage with null values because their Treasury budget homepages returned HTTP 403/503 to scripted access from this build environment; their figures must still be hand-keyed from a Budget Paper PDF opened in a browser.
fields.audit_method: Each Treasury budget homepage was probed for HTTP reachability from the build environment. Reachable (HTTP 200): NSW, VIC, QLD, WA budget homepages. Blocked from sandbox (HTTP 403/503): SA, TAS, ACT, NT budget homepages. In all eight cases, the actual net-debt figure lives in a Budget Paper PDF that requires browser access to verify, so values remain null in this scaffold.
